body: {"rationale":"Well-scoped task bead with clear context, concrete acceptance criteria, and explicit in/out-of-scope boundaries. Minor gaps: (1) no explicit test coverage requirement for the --json flag itself in acceptance criteria (only 'go test passes', not 'tests cover new JSON path'); (2) 'best-effort metadata' for local builds is underspecified — no guidance on precedence (build info vs git, fallback values if neither available); (3) acceptance criterion 2 tests --version human output but does not specify what 'non-empty commit metadata' means (hash length, format); (4) no mention of error behavior if git is unavailable in a non-repository context (e.g., installed binary outside a checkout).","score":88,"suggested_fixes":["Add an acceptance criterion explicitly testing the --json output schema: 'go run ./cmd/fiz version --json | jq -e .dirty' exits 0.","Specify fallback precedence for local build metadata: prefer git rev-parse HEAD, fall back to debug.ReadBuildInfo vcs.revision, fall back to 'unknown'.","Clarify commit format expectation (e.g., full 40-char SHA or short 7-char) to avoid ambiguity between implementations.","Add an out-of-scope or explicit behavior note for non-git environments (installed binary): 'commit may be empty string, dirty must be false'."],"waivers_applied":[]}
